channel.title: Posts on Ric - An unknown artist
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Ric - An unknown artist

item.title: German translation
item.description: I was just in the process of translating this page when events escalated , but now it’s done: This page is also a available in German . Additionally a page on the J.C. Blumenberg Kunstverlag has been added.

item.title: Alley - Ric20
item.description: Short description: Another print of Ric 20 Notes: Taken from eBay . The title of other works might be Treppengasse, but this should be considered to be not confirmed. Other entries for this work See also Ric 32

item.title: Another Fritz Neumann biography
item.description: Another “biography” (actually it’s a list of sold works) of a Fritz Neumann has surfaced, since it lists some works by “our” Fritz, it’s a it interesting. It lists his year of birth as around 1900 and claims that the year of death is 1930…

item.title: Ric on TV 2
item.description: After I had already noticed that Ric also had an appearance in Tatort, I asked the NDR as the producing broadcaster. The archive forwarded my enquiry directly to the production. Kerstin Ramcke, the producer of the Kieler Tatort, thankfully answered the enquiry directly. I am reproducing her answer here: The ‘Praesidium’ location was the military technical school in Kiel (Arkonastrasse). We filmed the offices at this location until 2017[…] Kerstin Ramcke Original in German: Der Drehort “Präsidium” war damals die Militärtechnische Schule in Kiel (Arkonastrasse).

item.title: Fritz Neumann (1928–2014) is not Ric!
item.description: Fritz Neumann (1928–2014) is not Ric! During my vacation I did some research on one of the last posts : I’ve contacted the Freudeskreis Kunst Oldenburg . They forwarded my questions to Andreas Neumann, the son of Fritz Neumann (1928–2014). He send me the following reply: Unfortunately, my father - Fritz Neumann - is not identical with the painter ‘Ric’. The handwriting and painting style do not match, and the Berlin address does not fit either.

item.title: Postcard by Fritz Neumann 2
item.description: Short description: A postcard by Fritz Neumann Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: Postcard by Fritz Neumann 1
item.description: Short description: A postcard by Fritz Neumann Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: Fritz Neumann Pferdekopf
item.description: Short description: A horse Notes: Taken from eBay Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts .

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 04)
item.description: Short description: Ships (fishing boats) at a harbour Notes: Found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: Fritz Neumann Boats
item.description: Short description: A colourised etching of boats by Fritz Neumann. Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: A new work of Ric discovered!
item.description: Short description: A previously unknown Ric showing the gallery “Zur Fischerin” in Lindau / Lake Constance Notes: Available on eBay . Current web site of “Zur Fischerin” , a similar view on Google Maps .

item.title: Fritz Neumann Terriers
item.description: Short description: A colourised print of terriers by Fritz Neumann. Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: Fritz Neumann Heron
item.description: Short description: A colourised etching of a heron by Fritz Neumann. Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: Fritz Neumann biographies
item.description: [Update 26. July 2024]: The solution of the mystery didn’t work out , this Fritz Neumann (1928–2014) isn’t Ric I’ve found two Fritz Neumann biographies and a change to the German Wikipedia: Freundeskreis Bildende Kunst Oldenburg e.V. By Dr. Arie Hartog of the Gerhard-Marcks-House Bremen: German original article Automatic English translation (by Google Translate) askART The full article requires a registration / subscription: Artist Biography & Facts: Fritz Neumann Wikipedia There also has been a change to the German Wikipedia, adding the Ric pseudonym to Fritz Neumann.

item.title: Fritz Neumann Poodle
item.description: Short description: A etching of a poodle by Fritz Neumann. Notes: Found on a fleamarket in Hannover for 15€

item.title: Sailing boat (Ric 07)
item.description: Short description: This is my third print of the Sailing boats (Ric 07 ). This print is interesting because it is not framed behind glass, but has been mounted on cardboard. The sheet has also been cropped to such an extent that not even the signature has been preserved. Notes: Found on a flea market in Hannover. Other entries for this work

item.title: Gerbrand Caspers' (quite) new project
item.description: Das Haus der Frau One of the experts helping me out from time to time is Gerbrand Caspers, also known as The Linosaurus , has started a new project. I’ve been following his work for about a decade and his colloction of woodcut prints is quite impressive. Please have a look at his new project website Das Haus der Frau , he also offeres a printed lexicon containing the results of his research.

item.title: Cover of Genossenschaftsfamilie Nr. 16 August 1935
item.description: Short description: This seems to be a cover of an issue of “Genossenschaftfamilie” with an painting by Fritz Neumann. It was a company magazine of the GEG (Deutsche Großeinkaufs-Gesellschaft) from Hamburg. Notes: More Information on the publication is available in German.

item.title: Another new Ric found by Mark Strong
item.description: Short description: Docked Boat and Glowing Lamp Post at Twilight Size with wide margins 25-1/16" (ca. 63cm) x 42-5/8" (ca. 108cm), Image size 15-5/8" (ca. 40cm) x 32-7/8" (ca. 83cm). Notes: Thanks to Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts for the hint, available on eBay . Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts .

item.title: A painting of Ric 20
item.description: Short description: This is a painting of the motive found on Ric 20 . The title is probably “Treppengasse”, which is in Altenburg , Germany. Since it’s a painting and not another copy of the etching, it get’s it’s own number (Ric 32). Notes: Found by Andra Robertson at an estate sale, send to me by mail. Image by Andra Robertson See also Ric 32

item.title: A new work of Ric discovered! Again by Mark Strong (Two of two)
item.description: Short description: Two swans in flight Notes: Thanks to Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts for the hint, available on eBay . Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . Other entries for this work

item.title: A new work of Ric discovered! Again by Mark Strong (One of two)
item.description: Short description: Three cormorans, one drying it’s wings Notes: Thanks to Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts for the hint, available on eBay Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . Other entries for this work

item.title: Fritz Neumann Birch Way
item.description: Short description: A path lined with birch trees, coastal ‘moorlands’ of sandy soil of North German Plain (Geest). Notes: On the back it’s noted that this was a gift in 1949.

item.title: The Ric mystery has been solved!
item.description: While doing the migration away from Tumblr and adding old drafts I also checked some links. The one pointing to the Google Image search showed two new Ric prints (see Ric 27 )and Ric 28 ). And as if that weren’t enough luck, I could hardly believe my eyes when I read the description: Someone not only claimed to have solved the Ric mystery, he also provided proof. Since I’m still in my migration project, I didn’t manage to update all affections sections and pages yet.

item.title: Moved to Projektemacher.org
item.description: This blog has been moved to Projektemacher . It’s now powered by Hugo and GitHub . There have been a few updates during this process: Images are now available in highest possible resolution An fullscreen view has been added Some old drafts are now public (over 33% more content - including a previously unknown work of Ric) Some more hints are available

item.title: Uncolourised version of Ric27
item.description: Short description: Another street scene with a lantern and a wind mill. The same seller also offerers a colourised version . Notes: Taken from eBay Images by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Yet yet another new work of Ric discovered!
item.description: Short description: A bridge - probably Pont des Arts , Paris Notes: Taken from eBay . The title of the listing suggests Pont des Arts, Paris. Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Alley - Ric20
item.description: Short description: An alley Notes: Taken from eBay . The title of the listing suggests Treppengasse, Altenburg , Germany. Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work See also Ric 32

item.title: Albergo - Ric24
item.description: Short description: An alley in front of a harbour Notes: Taken from eBay . The title of the listing suggests Milan , Italy. But Milan has no harbour… Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Sea Gulls - Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: Two sea gulls in flight Notes: Taken from eBay Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there will be a post about it.

item.title: Three Birds in Flight - Ric09
item.description: Short description: Three birds in flight Notes: Taken from eBay Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there will be a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Under the Sea - Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: Two colorized etchings of fishes and corals signed by Fritz Neumann. Notes: These can be found on eBay (listing 1 and listing 2 ) Images by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it.

item.title: Firebird - Ric18 - found on eBay
item.description: Short description: Firebird or “Feuervogel” Notes: Taken from eBay . Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Yet another new work of Ric discovered!
item.description: Short description: Another scene of ships in a harbour Notes: Taken from eBay . Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Another new work of Ric discovered!
item.description: Short description: Another street scene with a lantern and a wind mill (which isn’t red - as one can suspect if this should be Paris). Notes: Taken from eBay . Image by Mark Strong of Meibohm Fine Arts . The description on eBay is very interesting, there is a post about it. Other entries for this work

item.title: Another work of sailing boats by Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: Another work of sailing boats by Fritz Neumann. Notes: Found on eBay . There is a sticker on the back, but it’s unreadable. Interestingly, the picture is attributed to a Ric Neumann

item.title: Hanfstaengl-Drucke
item.description: A few day ago I bought an interesting book at my favorite rare book book seller : A catalog of deliverable prints of the “Kunstverlag Hanfstaengl” for the year 1961. The woman at the counter told me that the book has been sitting around on the shelf for ages and offered me to look up the price again, just in case the value dropped. Thy have tho policy to offer books at a lower price then the one on the web.

item.title: Good old Fritz in Paris
item.description: Short description: Paris, Montmartre with the Basilica of the Sacred Heart of Paris , commonly known as Sacré-Cœur Basilica in the background. Notes: Found by Wolfgang Barina on eBay Kleinanzeigen

item.title: Another version of the "JCB" blind stamp
item.description: Image by Christian Wolf - taken from Wikimedia Commons This is the probably older version of the blind stamp that’s used on some prints by Fritz Neumann. It imitates the Holsten Gate in Lübeck (second image), another indication of a North German origin. So far, inquiries from the Lübeck City Archives about former art publishers in the city have not yet been answered. For examples of the blind stamp in use, see:

item.title: Ric on TV
item.description: A few day ago I downloaded an episode of Tatort , a German TV series. The Episode “Tatort: Borowski und eine Frage von reinem Geschmack” - link in German only - aired on 24 October 2010. The story doesn’t really matter for this post, but have a look at the screenshot below, they have a Ric print hanging at the office. Screenshot of Borowski und eine Frage von reinem Geschmack

item.title: Another wrong attribution
item.description: Every regular reader will spot the error in the screen shot above really quick: This page is about Fritz Neumann (1881 – 1918) The second image shows a sailing boat, which is probably by another Fritz Neumann, especially since this one died 1918 and the construction of the „Laboe Naval Memorial“ , which can be seen in the background, was started in 1927. The building is also on my Brick expressionism blog … There is a higher resolution image available at veryimportantlot.

item.title: An answer from Rosenthal
item.description: A while a ago I’ve added a post showing a painted tile painted by Fritz Neumann and produced by Rosenthal . Since the company has quite a history they also offer a archive research service. In spring 2019 I’ve send them an inquiry about the history of this particular product and the artist, Today I got an answer. Since I don’t have their permission I don’t quote the reply here. The raw facts:

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 06)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Sold originally in Paderborn, see label. Other entries for this work

item.title: More sailing boats (Ric 05)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes : Taken from eBay . Other entries for this work

item.title: New work of Ric discovered!
item.description: Short description: A bird, maybe a Marabou Notes: Taken from eBay. Other entries for this work

item.title: Print of a foal by Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: A colourised print of a foal by Fritz Neumann Notes: Found on eBay

item.title: A clue from the old blog
item.description: Merry Christmas everyone! Finally it’s the time in year to eat cookies and do some personal research. I found the following comment on the original blog by Simon . I have over 30 different lithographs of the unknown artist RIC. Likewise I have in my collection 15 lithographs, which are signed with Fritz Neumann. The lithographs of both artists not only have the same embossing stamp “JCB” but show too clear similarities in motifs (boats, birds, lanes) as well as in brushstrokes.

item.title: A german shepherd (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: A German Shepherd Notes: Bought in Göttingen.

All hints of the original blog are incorporated now. The next steps are to wait for some replies and to find out more about the Holsten Gate blind stamp. I’ll continue to post new images of Ric’s works, when I find them. Until then the frequency of new entries will be quite low.

item.title: Large sailing ship by Ric (Ric 25)
item.description: Short description: Large sailing ship Notes : Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 10)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Taken from eBay . Other entries for this work

item.title: An etching of a wind mill by (some) Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: An etching of a wind mill by (some) Fritz Neumann Notes: Taken from eBay , probably not “our” Fritz Neumann.

item.title: Two prints by Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: Two prints by Fritz Neumann, sailings boats and a black-headed gull. Notes: Works by our Fritz Neumann wit typical maritime subjects.

item.title: A postcard by Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: A postcard by Fritz Neumann Notes: Found on Ansichtskarten-Center

item.title: The different signatures of our Fritz Neumann
item.description: One possibility to distinguish between our different Fritz Neumann’s is to compare the signature. The following list shows examples of Fritz Neumanns signatures, the source images are linked in the image caption: Signature our Fritz Neumann, taken from this post Signature our Fritz Neumann, taken from this post Signature our Fritz Neumann, taken from this post Signature our Fritz Neumann, taken from this post

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 07)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boats Notes: Taken from auctionet.com . Slightly different colouring then other versions . Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 07)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boats Notes: Slightly different colouring then other versions . Other entries for this work

item.title: A stair alley (Ric 14)
item.description: Short description: A stair (alley) Notes: Better quality then the first entry for this . Other entries for this work

item.title: Two prints from one plate (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: Two prints from one plate, another fox terrier. Notes: The first is taken from the Linosaurus Blog . Note the mark under the signature, Holsten Gate variant. The second is taken from Cofart.be . The colourisation above the eye is different. Note that the signature is different and it has a title (”Ali Baba”), no stamp mark visible.

item.title: A kitten by Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: A kitten Notes: Taken from eBay . This on got the Holsten Gate variant of the mark - image of it is lost.

item.title: A dachshund (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: A dachshund Notes: Taken from Cofart.be . The stamp mark is clearly visible.

item.title: Two dogs - fox terrier (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: Two dogs, fox terrier. Notes: Taken from eBay . This one is quite interesting, it’s attributed to Hermann Fritz Neumann and should be quite old. It also features the stamp mark, but in another form: The letters “J”, “C” and “B” are not overlapping, and are put in the middle of the Holstentor .

item.title: Dürers "Praying Hands" - Fritz Neumann
item.description: Short description: An etching after Albrecht Dürer’s “Preying Hands” by Fritz Neumann. Even if the motif and style doesn’t match, the signature does. Notes: No blind stamp visible.

item.title: Sailing boats in a harbour (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short Description: Sailing boats in a harbour / near a shore Notes: Another maritime subject by Fritz Neumann, on the back is a sticker reading “Echt Aquarell” (Original aquarelle). But this is clearly a colourised print, since there is a platemark.

item.title: This is the stamp mark used on the prints of Ric
item.description: This is the stamp mark (blind stamp) used on the prints of Ric and Fritz Neumann. There is now a dedicated page on this trace. It will be updated in the next few days with more examples and traces. You can also look at the pages tagged with “Blind embossing” .

item.title: Another work by our Fritz Neumann
item.description: Description: Another work by our Fritz Neumann, look at the signature and the stamp mark on the third picture. Notes: Taken from eBay Kleinanzeigen

item.title: Am Wind (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: A tile with a print called “Am Wind” made by Rosenthal Notes: Found on lauritz.com . There are many other examples available on the net (Google Image Search ). According to this page , the mark shown had been used by Rosenthal between 1939 and 1956. Another hint from this page is confirming this by the used style of the underline. Update: Rosenthal answered my inquiry

item.title: A signature of Fritz Neumann (1881 - 1919)
item.description: A signature of Fitz Neumann (1881 - 1919), taken from findartinfo.com . His subjects are mostly Cossacks, he was a painter.

item.title: Sailing boat (Ric 05)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boat Notes: See Antik Bayreuth Other entries for this work

item.title: Additional photos uploaded
item.description: Additional photos from different sources (like eBay and Google Image Search) have been added, the “About” section has been updated. Links to external sites (except eBay) had been added to the Wayback Machine . The next steps are the different known traces like the stamp mark and the different Fritz Neumanns.

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 06)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Sold originaly in Bremerhaven, see label. The shop still seem to exist. Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats by Ric (Ric 16)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boats Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Alley with three lamps (Ric 12)
item.description: Short description Alley with three lamps Notes: The name of the images is “Drei Lampen Gasse, Paris” (Three latern alley, Paris). One of only a few named prints currently known. Other entries for this work

item.title: Three boats (Ric 06)
item.description: Short description: Three boats Notes: Found on eBay and on Worthpoint Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boat next to a harbour (Ric 08)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boat next to a harbour / slip Notes: Found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing ship (Ric 03)
item.description: Short description: Sailing ship next to a harbour / town Notes: Found on eBay. Similar to Ric 17 , but this one is signed a bit different. Other entries for this work

item.title: Four sailing boats (Ric 22)
item.description: Short description: Four sailing boats (Spinnaker ) Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 10)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 05)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boats (Ric 05) Notes: Gefunden bei eBay. Other entries for this work

item.title: Three birds (Ric 09)
item.description: Short description: Three birds Notes: See Antik Bayreuth Other entries for this work

item.title: Two sailings boats (Ric 16)
item.description: Short description: Two sailings boats Notes: Found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: List of works imported
item.description: The list of works by “Ric” has been copied from the original page . They are accessible via tags from the “Known works” page, that way future posts will be assigned to the according entry automatically. The posts with contents from the original blog had been tagged with “Taken from Blogspot” . Also the some of the texts (About , Ric ) are finished.

item.title: Ric 24
item.description: Short description: An alley in front of a harbour Notes: Three images found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 25
item.description: Short description: A large sailing ship Notes: Found on eBay Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 23
item.description: Short description: Fishing net hung up to dry in the setting sun Notes: According to the description this is painted in oil on wood, around 1970, 66 x 34 cm See Lotsearch Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 22
item.description: Short description: Three sailing boats (spinnaker) Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 21
item.description: Short description: The Gedächniskirche in Berlin Notes: Quite unusual motive. Probably seen from Tauentzienstraße . Also see this picture from 1965 and look at the laterns Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 20
item.description: Short description: An alley Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 19
item.description: Short description: Sailing ship next to a harbour / town Notes: Similar to Ric 03 , but this one is signed a bit different. Maybe this one isn’t a print? Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 18
item.description: Short description: A marabu Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 17
item.description: Short description: Two sailings boats Notes: Similar to Ric 02 Similar to Ric 07 Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 16
item.description: Short description: Two sailings boats Notes: Higher resolution images are available Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 15
item.description: Short description: People on the shore next to a sailing boat Notes: Unusual rounded style, low image quality Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 14
item.description: Short description: A latern next to a stair (alley) Notes: Low quality image Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 13
item.description: Short description: Two birds Notes: Image has a very low quality Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 12
item.description: Short description: Alley with three lamps Notes: There seem to be no title on the lower left, but the image is cropped Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 11
item.description: Short description: Fishing nets and a ship Notes: No better image available Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 10
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boat Notes: Higher resolution images are available Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 09
item.description: Short description: Three birds Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 08
item.description: Short description: Sailing boat next to a harbour / slip Notes: Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 07
item.description: Short description: Two sailings boats Notes: Similar to Ric 02 Similar to Ric 17 Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 06
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Also quite common Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 05
item.description: Short description: Sailing boat Notes: This is one of the most common prints, see Ric 05 Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 04
item.description: Short description: Ships (fishing boats) at a harbour Notes: Better image available . Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 02
item.description: Short description: Two sailings boats Notes: Similar to Ric 07 Similar to Ric 17 Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 03
item.description: Short description: Sailing ship next to a harbour / town Notes: Similar to Ric 17 , but this one is drawn a bit different. Other entries for this work

item.title: Ric 01
item.description: Short description: Two sailing boats (Spinnaker ) Notes: Other entries for this work
